marketState: neutral, pulled between bearish storage/seasonality and a crowded short COT setup
summary: Price ($2.915) sits almost exactly on fair value ($2.923, band $2.423-$3.423), so there is no mispricing edge to trade right now. Seasonality is weak (price -7.5pp below its typical-year path, only 38% odds of being higher in 3-4 weeks) but reliability is explicitly flagged Low, so this shouldn't be weighted heavily. Storage is +180 Bcf above the 5-year average, a bearish backdrop, while managed-money COT positioning is near its most net-short level in 5 years (8th percentile), a crowded-short setup that raises squeeze risk on any bullish catalyst. Technicals are split: the shortest timeframes (5m/15m) show a bullish trend tag but fading (negative) MACD momentum, while 1d/1w show a clean bearish trend with RSI in the high-30s and 50<200 EMA crosses. Critically, both near-term weather (1-7D, 8-16D) and the futures curve are null in this snapshot, which are top-priority inputs for the short-term call, so conviction is kept low and both trades default to WAIT.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality leans slightly bearish (below-median YTD, -1.4% typical 4-week move, only 38% odds higher at 3-4 weeks) but is tagged Low reliability, and the +180 Bcf storage surplus vs 5y adds bearish weight. That's offset by managed money sitting at the 8th percentile of 5y net-short positioning -- a crowded short that raises squeeze risk -- and weather demand still +7% above 5y normal even though the 24h trend and the 8-16D outlook (null) can't confirm persistence.
longTerm.invalidation: A clean break below the $2.423 fair-value floor with storage surplus continuing to widen would confirm bearish continuation; a break above $3.423 with the COT short building further would flag a squeeze already underway.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for weather.demand_8_16D and the plus7D/14D/21D fair-value projections to populate, and for price to actually test one of the $2.42 / $3.42 band edges before committing directionally.

shortTerm.rationale: Price vs fair value shows no edge (front spread -$0.008, status FAIR). Technicals disagree across timeframes: 5m/15m tag bullish but with fading negative MACD, 1h is neutral with a bearish EMA cross, and 4h/1d/1w all show bearish trend with 1d/1w RSI in the high-30s -- no clean alignment to trade. Both demand_1_7D weather and the futures curve, top short-term priority inputs, are null this cycle.
shortTerm.invalidation: A confirmed flip of 1d/1w structure (RSI back above 50, EMA golden cross) would argue for longs; a decisive close outside the $2.42-$3.42 fair-value band on rising volume would confirm the next directional leg.
shortTerm.trigger: Wait for weather.demand_1_7D and futuresCurve data to populate, or for the 1h/4h timeframes to align with the 1d/1w bearish trend (or reverse it) before sizing a directional trade.

keyDrivers: Managed-money COT net short at 8th percentile of 5y range -- crowded short, squeeze risk
Storage +180 Bcf vs 5y average -- bearish supply backdrop
Price essentially at fair value ($2.915 vs $2.923 fair, FAIR status) -- no mispricing edge
Seasonality below median but explicitly Low reliability (59pp 10-90 spread)
Weather demand +7% vs 5y normal but falling 24h and near low of its own 7-day range
risks: Near-term weather (1-7D, 8-16D) and futures curve data are null this snapshot -- a real blind spot for timing either trade
